gate_claim: after the guard-hardening repair no checkout posture and no falsified base_branch can bind the discard-archive commit to a non-surviving branch, and no concurrent HEAD re-point inside the resolution-to-commit window can produce a false committed true or a false receiving-branch disclosure: the outcome is always either a commit verified on the surviving base branch or a truthfully reported not-committed with the receiving branch disclosed and residue recoverable, and every previously-surviving matrix cell (both F1 triggers, commit success, diff-quiet race, commit failure, offline, honest-state postures, preflight classification, four-edition parity) still holds
gate_surface: the release-to-discard-to-sibling-sink matrix re-run against the epoch-3 candidate with the N5-A falsified-state cells (release detached with base_branch 'HEAD', release on the feature branch with base_branch naming the discarded branch, sweep detached with 'HEAD', sweep on an arbitrary lane with base_branch naming that lane), the N5-B race cell (a git shim re-pointing HEAD between staging and commit), both F1 triggers, and the full prior matrix across all four edition copies
gate_aggregation: sequence
upstream_read: n4-security-certify-hardening 863394094486

verdict: pass
findings_blocking: 0
summary: both epoch-2 refutations re-run end-to-end against the repaired candidate are genuinely closed (all four N5-A falsified-base cells now refuse before staging with zero commits anywhere; the N5-B RC1 race downgrades to committed:false disclosing the actual receiver at BOTH call sites), and 135 own executed checks plus every shipped suite found no surviving counterexample.

## Candidate and context

- Candidate = the 14-file accumulated worktree diff vs HEAD in .kw/worktrees/issue-715 (verified: git rev-parse HEAD == claim_root_base cd28e8e52fb641cf2173ced57c91a042e3c13e1e). Candidate digest independently recomputed with the shipped computeLandableTreeDigest (scripts/kaola-workflow-validation-runner.js:507): af7e553c1fc7f26435278ba7695b6c0d6135fd95b24d7d2e8da310a9c9bb68da — byte-identical to the dispatch and review context. The 14 modified files are exactly the claim.js/sink-merge.js four-edition family, scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js, scripts/test-claim-hardening.js, scripts/test-sink-merge.js, CHANGELOG.md, docs/api.md, docs/workflow-state-contract.md, plus the untracked kaola-workflow/issue-715/ run tree — zero out-of-set writes.
- Review context honored structurally: validation_obligations [] (nothing extra owed), review_phase discovery, attempt_ordinal 1, prior_findings [] — recorded. Read first, in order: the epoch-2 refutation n5-falsify-branch-commit 7e6482a523a9 (from kaola-workflow/issue-715/.cache/epochs/2/files/.cache/ — the nominal path is gone), the issue body (gh issue view 715), n1 producer evidence (f6077183028a), n3 code certification (212afaa15a1f), n4 security certification (863394094486 — nonce matches the upstream_read token), and the epoch-1 parent falsification (for O2's exact wording).
- Repair examined in full before attacking: commitDiscardArchive hardened inside the helper (scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:2364-2469) — clause (a) sentinel rejection (:2389-2393), (b) argument-array rev-parse --verify refs/heads/<base> (:2394-2406), (c) discardedBranch refusal (:2407-2413), (d) sweep defaultBase constraint (:2414-2422), retained epoch-2 equality (:2423-2427), all before the first mutating call (:2429); N5-B post-commit re-resolution (:2444-2453) + merge-base --is-ancestor (:2454-2461, fail-closed) + retained cat-file presence check (:2462-2464), all inside the never-throw try (:2466-2468). Call sites: release hoist + { discardedBranch: featureBranch } (:3422/:3464-3465) + loud warnings[] (:3489-3492) + unconditional branch disclosure (:3496-3499); sweep pre-read + default fallback + { discardedBranch: folder.branch, defaultBase: sweepDefaultBase } (:4430-4438/:4452-4453) + cleanup-entry emit (:4491-4495).
- Role honored strictly read-only: no repository file created, modified, or deleted (git status --porcelain at session end is byte-identical to session start: the same 14 modified files + untracked run tree). All fixtures were built under $TMPDIR ($TMPDIR/kw715-n5e3/: common.js, driver-cells.js, driver-races.js, driver-helper-probes.js, driver-preflight.js, driver-issue-repro.js, driver-supplementary.js; git/gh shims, tmp repos). Every cell drove the REAL candidate scripts end-to-end via spawnSync (release/watch-pr/sink-merge CLI) or the REAL exported helper via require() — 135 executed checks total (77 + 21 + 12 + 4 + 8 + 13), all passing.

## N5-A re-run (epoch-2 refutation, four falsified-base cells) — RESOLVED at both call sites

Each cell re-run end-to-end exactly as the epoch-2 demonstrations built them; every one must now refuse or skip truthfully with no chore commit on any ref, the receiving branch disclosed, residue recoverable, and the archive never stranded.

- B4a (release, entry posture detached HEAD, base_branch falsified to 'HEAD'): exit 0 released:true, discard_archive_committed:false + discard_archive_branch:"HEAD" + detail 'recorded base branch "HEAD" is the detached-HEAD sentinel, not a real branch; refusing to commit…' — clause (a) fires. Detached tip AND main tip byte-unchanged; git log --all shows no chore subject on any ref (only 'init'); residue on disk (1 discarded dir) and manually committable on main afterwards (cat-file main:<rel> → tree after the manual commit). In epoch 2 this cell committed onto the detached HEAD and orphaned on natural cleanup; now nothing commits anywhere. RESOLVED.
- W5 (watch-pr CLOSED sweep, detached HEAD, base 'HEAD'): watched:1, cleanups[0].discard_archive_committed:false + branch "HEAD" + the same sentinel detail via the second call site; both tips byte-unchanged; no chore commit; residue recoverable. RESOLVED.
- B3 (release on the feature branch, base_branch naming the discarded branch workflow/issue-801): exit 0 released:true, committed:false + branch "workflow/issue-801" + detail 'names the branch being discarded' — clause (c) fires. Feature tip AND main tip byte-unchanged, no chore commit on any ref, the archive a tree at NEITHER ref, residue recoverable. RESOLVED.
- W6 (sweep on workflow/other-lane, base_branch naming that lane): cleanup entry committed:false + branch "workflow/other-lane" + detail 'is not the surviving default branch "main"' — only clause (d) can catch this cell (the lane is a real ref, not the folder's own lane, and equals the checkout) and it does, BEFORE staging: both ref tips byte-unchanged, no chore commit, residue recoverable. RESOLVED.

## N5-B re-run (RC1 race) plus two stronger race variants — RESOLVED

- RC1 (deterministic git shim on PATH interposing at the helper's `add -A -- <rel>`: real add, then symbolic-ref HEAD onto pre-created 'race' + checkout): exit 0 released:true (never stranded), discard_archive_committed:false with detail 'HEAD moved from "main" to "race" during the discard archive commit; the commit landed off-base on "race" (recoverable there)', discard_archive_branch:"race" — the ACTUAL receiver, with an explicit negative check that the emit never contains '"discard_archive_branch":"main"'. main tip byte-unchanged; the archive is NOT a tree at main (cat-file → fatal) and IS a tree at race with race tip subject 'chore: discard archive issue-801' — recoverable. The epoch-2 false disclosure (committed:true naming stale 'main') is eliminated. RESOLVED.
- RC1s (same shim on the SWEEP path): watched:1, cleanup entry downgrades identically — committed:false + branch "race" (never 'main'), main tip unchanged, archive recoverable at race. The fix is in-helper, so both call sites inherit it; proven, not inferred. RESOLVED at the second call site.
- RC1x (strictly stronger double-switch adversary: HEAD→race at add so the commit lands on race, HEAD→main again at commit so the re-resolution reads 'main'): the helper NEVER emitted committed:true — it failed closed through the retained cat-file presence check to { committed:false, branch:'main', detail:'discard archive commit failed: Command failed: git … cat-file -t HEAD:<rel>' }; main tip byte-unchanged, the commit verified a tree at race (recoverable), residue on disk. The second interposition sits between commit and re-resolution — outside the claim's resolution-to-commit window — and even there the durability claim cannot be falsified. Recorded as a non-blocking observation below (O-N3-1 family, matching n3/n4).
- RC3 (base rewound between commit and reachability probe — shim at commit: real commit on main, then update-ref refs/heads/main HEAD~1): committed:false (truthful: the archive is genuinely not at the rewound main tip), main back at the pre-commit tip, rev-list --all --objects -- <rel> EMPTY (chore commit dangling, recoverable via main reflog), residue on disk. Mechanism note, recorded analytically: because HEAD is a symref to main, the rewind moves HEAD's own resolution, so branchAfter==='main' still holds and merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD main is trivially satisfied (a ref is its own ancestor); the rewind class is actually caught by the retained cat-file presence check, with the merge-base clause serving as the fail-closed backstop for genuine HEAD/base divergence and probe errors. The outcome contract — never a false committed:true — held.

## Re-attacked epoch-2 survivors — all still hold (executed, not inferred)

- F1 trigger A (R1, in-place NATIVE-style release with checkout ON the feature branch, OFFLINE=1): exit 0 released:true, restore PROCEEDED (HEAD=main, feature branch deleted, no restore_note), discard_archive_committed:true + branch "main", archive a tree at main:<rel>, main tip = 'chore: discard archive issue-801', zero .discarded- residue in porcelain -uall, rev-list main -- <rel> non-empty (reachable from the surviving base). HOLDS.
- F1 trigger B (W3, watch-pr CLOSED sweep on workflow/other-lane): watched:1, cleanup entry committed:false + branch "workflow/other-lane" + detail naming the surviving base 'main', BOTH ref tips byte-unchanged, no chore commit, residue recoverable AND manually committable on main afterwards (cat-file → tree). HOLDS.
- Commit success on base: R0 (release from main) — committed:true on main, chore-commit tip, tree at main:<rel>, zero residue. W1 (CLOSED sweep on main) — committed:true + branch 'main', chore tip, tree at main:<rel>. HOLD.
- Diff-quiet race (RC2, shim committing the staged rel on main between add and commit): the helper's own commit skipped (no 'chore: discard archive' subject anywhere; the race commit present exactly once), committed:true + branch "main" with the tree at main — truthful, no duplicate, no spurious failure. HOLDS.
- Commit failure: R2a (pre-commit hook exit 1) — exit 0 released:true (never stranded), committed:false + branch "main" + 'discard archive commit failed: Command failed: git … commit …' detail + loud warnings[] entry, main tip unchanged, no chore commit, residue staged-recoverable ('A' entries in diff --cached). R2b (.gitignore defeats the add) — restore proceeded (the ignored dest is invisible to the -uall dirty gate; HEAD=main, feature deleted), committed:false + 'git add failed for <rel>: … paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files …' detail + warning, main unchanged, residue on disk. HOLD.
- Offline: every release fixture ran KAOLA_WORKFLOW_OFFLINE=1 (commits landed regardless); the helper body contains zero OFFLINE branches (grep count 0 — the commit that runs is never skipped for offline); a direct helper probe under KAOLA_WORKFLOW_OFFLINE=1 committed identically (P11). HOLDS.
- Honest-state postures: B1 (release detached, honest base 'main') → committed:false + branch "HEAD" + 'not the surviving base branch "main"' detail, both tips unchanged, residue. B2 (symbolic-ref branch: refs/heads/sym → refs/heads/main, HEAD → sym) → git resolves abbrev-ref through the chain to 'main'; committed:true on main, tree at main:<rel>, disclosure accurate. B4b (on the feature branch, base 'HEAD') → `git checkout HEAD` no-ops, clause (a) now refuses with the sentinel detail (stronger than epoch-2's equality mismatch — same truthful skip class), no commit anywhere, residue. W4 (sweep detached, honest base) → committed:false + branch "HEAD", tips unchanged, residue. B0 (new honest variant: release entered on an arbitrary lane, base 'main') → equality guard skips truthfully, both tips unchanged, residue. ALL HOLD.

## New directed attacks constructed against the repair (strongest falsification attempts) — none breaks the claim

- N1 (release entered on arbitrary lane-a with base_branch falsified to that same lane): the lane is a real SURVIVING branch and the operator's actual checkout — committed:true + branch "lane-a", feature branch deleted, tree at lane-a:<rel>, rev-list non-empty. The commit binds only to a surviving, accurately-disclosed branch; in-contract. Claim HOLDS.
- N2 (release on feature, base falsified to nonexistent 'ghost'): the restore's `git checkout ghost` fails (feature branch kept), clause (b) refuses — committed:false + 'does not name a real local branch', tips unchanged, residue. HOLDS.
- N3a (whitespace-padded '  HEAD  '): trimmed and rejected as the sentinel; no commit; HOLDS. N3b (lowercase 'head'): git's case-insensitive pseudo-ref resolution makes the restore's `git checkout head` DETACH HEAD (observed), then clause (b) still refuses ('refs/heads/head' is not a real branch) — committed:false, disclosed branch === the actual current branch ('HEAD'), main untouched, residue recoverable, no chore commit. Even git's pseudo-ref quirk cannot smuggle a commit through. HOLDS.
- N5 (honest sweep on a non-main default: origin/HEAD symref → trunk, base 'trunk', checkout trunk): clause (d) is not over-strict — committed:true + branch "trunk", tree at trunk:<rel>. HOLDS.
- N6 (clause-(d) trust boundary: refs/remotes/origin/HEAD planted at lane-z + base falsified to 'lane-z', checkout on lane-z): the sweep's default probe resolves lane-z and the commit lands there — committed:true + branch "lane-z", tree at lane-z:<rel>, reachable, main untouched. The boundary is exactly as n3 adjudicated: (d) trusts the repo's OWN ref configuration, never operator-controlled workflow-state; even at this boundary the commit binds only to a real, surviving, accurately-disclosed branch — never stranded, never misreported. The claim (surviving-branch binding + truthful reporting) HOLDS; recorded as a boundary demonstration, non-blocking.
- Helper-level probes P1–P13 against the REAL exported helper (require() from the candidate): tag lookalike 'v9' refused at (b); revsyntax 'main^{commit}' passes verify but refused by the retained equality guard; full-ref form 'refs/heads/main' refused; symref base 'sym' refused (abbrev-ref resolves through to 'main'); honest on-base commit with reachability + presence proven; dest outside the repo refused pre-mutation; dest == toplevel refused; sweep non-default live lane refused at (d) with tip unchanged; sweep honest default commits; empty base refused; OFFLINE=1 commits identically; a prefix-look-alike branch name ('workflow/issue-9-evil' vs discarded 'workflow/issue-9') is NOT over-refused by (c) when the operator genuinely sits on that surviving lane (exact-match clause); sweep clause-(c) refuses the folder's own lane. All fail in the CLOSED direction; none produces a false committed:true or a bind to a non-surviving branch.

## Preflight classification matrix — holds

- SINK_RECEIPT_EXEMPT regex line extracted from ALL FOUR sink-merge copies: present in each, byte-identical across copies (md5(line) 9263a48f244f314aafab312777a47e29).
- 23/23 classification probes as expected: own/sibling live receipts, archived receipt, and discarded-dir receipt exempt; .bak/.tmp suffixes, directory form, multi-segment nesting, non-receipt .cache files, case variants of the filename/extension, leading/trailing whitespace, trailing slash, double slashes, wrong root, and 'sink-receipt.jsonx' look-alike all refused; the two degenerate reserved-name forms still exempt (= parent O1, classification-only, unchanged).
- Behavioral sibling-blocking (from repro B's negative control): a non-receipt sibling file under kaola-workflow/archive/<sib>/.cache/ refuses sink_blocked naming exactly that file, with ZERO mutation (main tip byte-unchanged, no new commits) — the exemption did not weaken foreign-dirt refusal.

## Four-edition parity — proven, not inferred

- Codex twins: diff scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js EMPTY; same for sink-merge.js — byte-identical.
- Helper body md5 (my extraction: the full function commitDiscardArchive…} text) 206697f58eb16feedf195c92c623226a identical in canonical, gitlab, and gitea copies; exactly 3 commitDiscardArchive( occurrences per file (1 definition + 2 call sites) in all four trees — no missed or extra site.
- Every call-site hunk mirrored in both forge ports (gitlab :3140/:3158-3159/:3182-3183/:3212-3215/:3590-3598/:3612-3613/:3652-3655; gitea :3135/:3153-3154/:3177-3178/:3207-3210/:3585-3593/:3607-3608/:3647-3650): releaseBaseBranch hoist + restore usage + call with discardedBranch only; sweepBaseBranch pre-read + default fallback + sweepDefaultBase + call with both opts; unconditional branch disclosure + detail-on-failure emits.
- node scripts/validate-script-sync.js → OK (25 common scripts, 28 byte-identical groups, 8 rename-normalized families, 7 forge export-superset families in sync); node scripts/edition-sync.js --check → parity (12 forge aggregator ports, 25 COMMON_SCRIPTS mirrors, 28 byte-identical groups).

## Issue's two reproductions, run end-to-end against the candidate

- Repro (a) (release leaves discard archive untracked → next sink refuses foreign_dirt): planted an active claim, ran the REAL claim.js release OFFLINE — released:true, discard_archive_committed:true on main, porcelain -uall shows zero .discarded- residue; then the REAL sink-merge --sink for another project proceeded (no sink_blocked, foreign_dirt []) and completed status:'sinked' with all steps done. The original failure mode (manual commit required to unblock the sink) is gone.
- Repro (b) (interrupted sink leaves its own receipt): planted kaola-workflow/archive/issue-870/.cache/sink-receipt.json as untracked dirt at the main root; a DIFFERENT project's REAL sink-merge did not refuse on the receipt and completed status:'sinked'. Negative control: replacing the receipt with a non-receipt sibling file refuses sink_blocked naming that file, zero mutation. The exempt-exactly-the-receipt / block-everything-else boundary holds.

## Observation re-probes (non-blocking; none worsened by the repair)

- O-N5-1 (silent removeBranch swallow): re-probed inside B3 — the restore's removeBranch of the CURRENT feature branch still fails silently (no restore_note; the pre-existing catch at claim.js:3449 untouched) and the feature branch survives. The repair neither fixed nor worsened the swallow, but the emit now discloses the clause-(c) refusal detail naming the discarded branch, so the posture is MORE visible than in epoch 2. Non-blocking stands.
- O-N5-2 / parent O1 (reserved-name receipt paths): both degenerate forms (kaola-workflow/archive/.cache/sink-receipt.json, kaola-workflow/.cache/.cache/sink-receipt.json) still match the exemption regex — classification-only, zero mutation impact, unchanged from the parent epoch. Non-blocking stands.
- Epoch-1 O2 (gitignored-residue warning phrasing): re-probed inside R2b — the warnings[] entry still ends '…commit it manually or the next sink preflight will refuse it as foreign dirt.' while a gitignored residue is invisible to the preflight (and a manual add needs -f). Same imprecision as epoch 1, not worsened; the discard_archive_commit_detail field itself remains accurate (quotes git's 'paths are ignored' error). Non-blocking stands.
- O-N3-1 family (RC1x double-switch): under an adversary strictly stronger than RC1 (HEAD re-pointed away for the commit AND back before the re-resolution), the helper fails closed via cat-file to committed:false; the failure-path branch field names the current (non-receiving) branch 'main' — the same shape as every other failure path (B1/R2a) — while the off-base commit is recoverable on 'race' and the residue on disk. The durability claim can never be falsified this way; the second interposition is outside the claim's resolution-to-commit window. Non-blocking stands (matches n3's O-N3-1 and n4's residual).
- RC3 mechanics note: the base-rewind class is caught by the retained cat-file presence check rather than the merge-base clause (HEAD resolves through the rewound symref, so branchAfter===base and the ancestor probe is trivially satisfied); the merge-base clause remains a fail-closed backstop (any probe error → downgrade). The outcome contract held in every interleave; classification-only.

## Shipped suites re-run by this reviewer in the worktree (corroboration, all exit 0)

- node scripts/test-claim-hardening.js → 'claim-hardening tests passed (485 assertions)' (464 epoch-2 + 21 new N5-A/N5-B pins).
- node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js → 'Workflow walkthrough simulation passed' (full suite), and each of the eight targeted scenarios passed standalone via --only: testReleaseDetachedHeadLyingBaseSkipsArchiveCommit, testReleaseOnFeatureBranchLyingBaseNamesDiscardedBranchSkips, testWatchPrClosedSweepDetachedLyingBaseHeadSkips, testWatchPrClosedSweepArbitraryLaneLyingBaseSkips, testReleaseHeadRepointRaceDowngradesArchiveCommit, testReleaseInPlaceOnFeatureBranchCommitsArchiveOnBase, testWatchPrClosedSweepSkipsCommitOffBaseBranch, testSinkForeignDirtExemptsSiblingReceipt715.
- node scripts/test-sink-merge.js → 105 assertions passed (incl. the #715 (l) over-exemption guard and (m) own-receipt regression lock); node scripts/test-bundle-finalize.js → all 149 passed; node scripts/test-kimi-edition.js → 577 assertions; node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js → 547 assertions; node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-sinks.js and node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-sinks.js → both passed.

## Gate verdict

The two epoch-2 counterexamples do not survive the repair. N5-A: all four falsified-base cells (B4a/W5/B3/W6) now refuse BEFORE staging at both call sites — zero commits on any ref (byte-unchanged tips + no chore subject in git log --all), the branch disclosed on every emit, the archive left as recoverable residue and manually committable on base; the sentinel, discarded-branch, nonexistent-ref, padded/lowercase, tag, revsyntax, full-ref, symref, and arbitrary-lane base classes are all refused. N5-B: the RC1 race downgrades to committed:false disclosing the ACTUAL receiver 'race' — never the stale 'main' — at BOTH call sites, and the strictly stronger double-switch and base-rewind variants still cannot produce a false committed:true (the retained cat-file check fails closed). Every epoch-2 surviving cell held under re-attack: both F1 triggers, commit success on base at both call sites, the diff-quiet race (truthful true, no duplicate), commit failure (hook exit, gitignored dest — loud, never stranding), offline (zero OFFLINE branches in the helper; commits land under OFFLINE=1), the honest-state postures (B0/B1/B2/B4b/W4), the full preflight classification matrix (23/23 + sibling non-receipt blocking with zero mutation + byte-identical regex in four copies), and four-edition parity (byte twins, md5-identical helper, mirrored call sites, both sync validators green). The issue's two reproductions run clean end-to-end. New directed attacks (N1 surviving-lane bind, N2 ghost base, N3 sentinel variants, N5/N6 default-boundary, P1–P13 helper probes) all fail closed or stay in-contract. Four pre-existing observations re-probed: unchanged, non-blocking, none worsened. Per the inverted burden, the strongest counterexamples I could construct in every directed class failed to break the gate_claim as written. Confidence: high — every adjudication is an executed end-to-end demonstration against the real candidate (135 own checks), not code reading.

domain_outcome: not_refuted
claim_outcome: not_refuted
